job_description.title: Role & Key Responsibilities
job_description.subtitle: Radiologic Technology Specialist | Diagnostic & Interventional Medical Imaging Specialist | Radiation Protection Officer (RPO)
job_description.summary_text: A highly experienced Radiologic Technology Specialist with expertise in diagnostic and interventional medical imaging, radiation protection, and quality management. Responsible for performing and optimizing advanced imaging procedures while ensuring compliance with radiation safety regulations, minimizing radiation risks, and maintaining the highest standards of patient and occupational safety.

imaging.title: Diagnostic & Interventional Medical Imaging
imaging.subtitle: Clinical Procedures & Imaging Modalities
items: Perform and supervise a wide range of diagnostic and interventional imaging procedures according to clinical protocols and international standards.
Assist radiologists and medical teams during interventional procedures by providing accurate imaging guidance.
Optimize imaging protocols to achieve high-quality diagnostic images with appropriate radiation dose management.
Ensure patient preparation, positioning, safety, and comfort throughout imaging procedures.
Maintain accurate clinical documentation and imaging records.

radiation.title: Radiation Protection & Regulatory Responsibilities
radiation.subtitle: RPO Compliance & Nuclear Safety
items: Act as Radiation Protection Officer (RPO) responsible for implementing radiation safety programs and ensuring compliance with national and international radiation protection standards.
Develop and monitor radiation protection policies according to ALARA principles (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).
Conduct radiation risk assessments and support the implementation of corrective and preventive actions.
Monitor occupational and patient radiation exposure and recommend dose optimization strategies.
Ensure proper use, maintenance, and calibration of radiation monitoring devices and personal dosimeters.
Perform radiation safety inspections and verify compliance with radiation protection requirements.
Ensure safe operation of X-ray generating equipment and radioactive imaging systems.
Participate in licensing, regulatory compliance, and communication with radiation control authorities.
Provide radiation safety training and awareness programs for healthcare staff.
Investigate radiation incidents and implement measures to prevent recurrence.

qa.title: Quality Assurance & Clinical Excellence
qa.subtitle: Quality Management & Multidisciplinary Care
items: Implement Quality Assurance (QA) programs for medical imaging equipment and clinical workflows.
Monitor image quality, equipment performance, and protocol optimization.
Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance safety, efficiency, and patient care.
Collaborate with radiologists, medical physicists, engineers, and multidisciplinary healthcare teams.

showcase.title: Clinical Practice Highlights
showcase.subtitle: Real Imaging & Interventional Procedures Handled in Professional Practice
showcase.click_hint: Click image to view high-resolution case details

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Radiologic Technology Specialist | Diagnostic & Interventional Medical Imaging | Licensed Radiation Protection Officer
about.biography: I am a Radiologic Technology Specialist with a Bachelor’s Degree in Diagnostic and Interventional Radiologic Technology and extensive experience in medical imaging and healthcare.
about.mission_p1: As a Radiologic Technology Specialist specializing in Diagnostic & Interventional Medical Imaging and Licensed Radiation Protection Officer, my mission is to advance healthcare by delivering excellence in diagnostic and interventional imaging while ensuring the highest standards of radiation protection and nuclear safety.
about.mission_p2: I am committed to promoting patient safety, supporting healthcare professionals, and contributing to the safe and effective use of ionizing radiation through professional practice, continuous learning, quality improvement, and compliance with international standards.
about.mission_p3: With expertise in advanced medical imaging technologies and image-guided procedures, I strive to optimize diagnostic accuracy, enhance clinical workflows, and support the delivery of safe, efficient, and patient-centered healthcare services.
about.mission_tagline: Radiologic Technology Specialist | Diagnostic & Interventional Medical Imaging | Licensed Radiation Protection Officer

expertise.title: Areas of Expertise
expertise.subtitle: Advanced Medical Imaging, Interventional Procedures & Radiation Safety Competencies

experience.title: Professional Experience
experience.subtitle: Professional Certifications, Radiation Safety Training & Advanced Medical Imaging Education

exp_01.title: Radiation Protection Officer (RPO)
responsibilities: Implemented radiation protection principles and safety procedures in accordance with national and international standards.
Monitored radiation safety practices and ensured compliance with regulations for the safe use of ionizing radiation.
Supported radiation risk assessment, dose monitoring, and occupational radiation safety programs.
Participated in quality assurance activities and safety inspections related to radiation protection.
Promoted awareness and training on radiation safety practices among healthcare professionals.
Ensured adherence to ALARA principles to minimize unnecessary radiation exposure.

exp_02.title: Radiology Center Manager | Radiation Protection Officer | Quality Manager
exp_02.institution: Al-Hayat Center for Diagnostic & Interventional Radiology – Karbala, Iraq
responsibilities: Managed all clinical and operational activities of the Diagnostic & Interventional Radiology Center.
Supervised MRI, CT, X-ray, Ultrasound, Fluoroscopy, and Interventional Radiology services.
Directed radiation protection and ensured compliance with international radiation safety standards.
Led quality assurance programs and continuous quality improvement initiatives.
Managed multidisciplinary teams and optimized workflow, operational efficiency, and patient care.
Supported equipment performance, staff training, and implementation of best clinical practices.

exp_03.title: Radiology Department Manager | Radiation Protection Officer | Quality Manager
exp_03.institution: Warith International Hospital for Oncology – Karbala, Iraq
responsibilities: Managed daily operations of the Radiology Department.
Led radiology staff and coordinated imaging services for oncology patients.
Served as Radiation Protection Officer (RPO), ensuring compliance with radiation safety regulations.
Managed the Quality Management System (QMS) within the radiology department.
Implemented quality improvement initiatives and optimized clinical workflows.
Supervised diagnostic imaging services while maintaining patient safety and regulatory compliance.

exp_04.title: Radiologic Technology Specialist
exp_04.institution: Nile Scan Radiology Center – New Cairo (Fifth Settlement), Egypt
responsibilities: Performed and supervised diagnostic imaging across multiple modalities.
Responsible for MRI, CT, PET/CT, Digital X-ray, Mammography, Dental Imaging, and Angiography services.
Ensured radiation protection compliance and optimized imaging protocols.
Maintained quality assurance (QA) standards and equipment performance.
Collaborated with radiologists and multidisciplinary teams to provide accurate diagnostic services.

exp_05.title: Radiologic Technologist
exp_05.institution: Tanta University Hospitals (Emergency Hospital & International Educational French Hospital)
responsibilities: Performed diagnostic radiographic examinations in emergency, inpatient, and outpatient settings.
Worked in both the Emergency Hospital and the International Educational French Hospital of Tanta University Hospitals.
Assisted physicians in emergency and specialized imaging procedures.
Applied radiation protection principles and ensured patient safety according to international standards.
Operated diagnostic imaging equipment while maintaining high image quality and workflow efficiency.
Completed clinical application training (Application Specialist) on Siemens Healthineers medical imaging systems.
